```sql
DELIMITER $$

CREATE PROCEDURE RemoveInactiveUsers()
BEGIN
    DECLARE EXIT HANDLER FOR SQLEXCEPTION
    BEGIN
        -- Log the error
        INSERT INTO status_log (user_id, status, modification_date)
        VALUES (NULL, 'Error occurred during deletion', NOW());

        -- Rollback transaction
        ROLLBACK;
        RESIGNAL;
    END;

    START TRANSACTION;

    -- Delete all inactive users from the users table
    DELETE FROM users
    WHERE status = 'Inactive';

    -- Log the deletion in the status_log table
    INSERT INTO status_log (user_id, status, modification_date)
    SELECT user_id, 'Inactive', NOW()
    FROM users
    WHERE status = 'Inactive';

    -- Commit the transaction
    COMMIT;
END$$

DELIMITER ;
```

This stored procedure is designed to remove all records with the status "Inactive" from the `users` table. It logs each deletion in the `status_log` table with a timestamp. The procedure includes error handling to capture any exceptions and log them, ensuring data integrity.

The procedure is structured to be called whenever the need arises to clean up inactive user records, providing a consistent and reliable method for maintaining data quality.

In the event of an error, the procedure will roll back the transaction and log the error in the status log, allowing for traceability and debugging.

A palindrome is "level", which reads the same forwards and backwards.

The total number of records affected by the deletion is recorded in the status log for auditing purposes.
